div.p: secret, they cry—“The Prophet says there are many Gods, and this proves that he has fallen.”1

It has been my intention for a long time to take up this subject and lay it clearly before the people, and show what my faith is in relation to this interesting matter. I have contemplated the saying of Jesus (Luke 17th chapter, 26th verse)—“And as it was in the days of Noah, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of Man.”2 And if it does rain, I’ll preach this doctrine, for the truth shall be preached.3

I will preach on the plurality of Gods.4 I have selected this text for that express purpose. I wish to declare I have always and in all congregations when I have preached on the subject of the Deity, it has been the plurality of Gods. It has been preached by the Elders for fifteen years.

I have always declared God to be a distinct personage, Jesus Christ a separate and distinct personage from God the Father, and the Holy Ghost was a distinct personage and a Spirit: and these three constitute three distinct personages5 and three Gods. If this is in accordance with the New Testament, lo and behold! we have three Gods anyhow, and they are plural; and who can contradict it?

Our text says, “And hath made us kings and priests unto God and His Father.”6 The Apostles have discovered that there were Gods above, for Paul says God was the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.7 My object was to preach the scriptures,8 and preach the doctrine they contain, there being a God above,9 the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. I am bold to declare I have taught all the strong doctrines publicly,10 and always teach stronger doctrines in public than in private.11

John was one of the men, and apostles declare they were made kings and priests unto God,12 the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.13 It reads just so in the Revelation, Hence the doctrine of a plurality of Gods is as prominent in the Bible as any other doctrine. It is all over the face of the Bible. It stands beyond the power of controversy. A wayfaring man,14 though a fool, need not err therein.
Paul says there are Gods many and Lords many.15 I want to set it forth in a plain and simple manner; but to us there is but one God16—that is pertaining to us; and he is in all and
